passages.excerpt:
September 15, 1976

Dear Dorothy,

I wanted to add a footnote to my earlier letter about my religious discoveries: new light shed on them through an interview with a lady reporter who came to see me yesterday. I mentioned to her that 36 of my novels asked the question, What is real? which is the basic question of all Greek philosophy, starting with Parmenides who felt that what we see is "dokos" or a kind of counterfeit reality, behind which lies a real or absolute reality; this distinction is later found in Kant. I told the lady reporter that my newest novel, VALISYSTEM A, is not this question repeated but the answer given, and she pointed out that after 25 years I seem to have arrived at an answer. For the first time I realized that my religious experience of 1974 was indeed an answer to a 25 year seeking, and not mere happeenstance, and I saw it in perspective for the first time. That my answer should be the Christian answer did not appear to disturb the lady reporter, but she pointed out that those fans who had grumbled at my asking the question, "What is real?" repeatedly would probably now grumble at the answer which I had found, which was a blend of Platonism, Neoplatonism, Gnosticism and Christianity, with nothing really new in it. Later I got to thinking of how many factors had gone into my finding this answer. I had just had several teeth removed, and was still under the influence of sodium pentathol, for one thing. As a second major factor, I was experimenting with the orthomolecular vitamin formula, and had accidentally taken 7 grams of vitamin C, much more than is indicated. Thirdly, a girl had come to my door with a gold fish necklace, which had fascinated me, and she had said, "This is a sign used by the early Christians." Also, I own a very old wooden saint figure, and for some reason had been moved to burn a votive candle before it perpetually. Too, it was the time of the vernal equinox, which is near Easter and the Jewish Passover. All these factors were causal in producing what happened; neural firing took place in the right hemisphere of my brain for the first time, and there was a radical drop in GABA fluid, which is the inhibiting fluid, allowing metasystems to fire for the first time (I use Maslow's terms here). The experience did not come easily, and, as I mentioned in my earlier letter, I was in a unique state of fear and dread. My first stage of the experience was to undergo the Bardo Thodol journey, and was then suddenly surprised to find myself confronting Aphrodite, who Empedocles believed to be the generative principle of all life, of all love and the formation of krasis or gestalts in the universe, as opposed to the principle of strife. What this meant I did not know at the time. Soon thereafter I was treated to 8 hours of violent phosphene activity, resembling modern nonobjective graphics such as those by Klee and Kandinsky; at this point I was aware that another mind was reaching out to my own, but I thought it human, perhaps a Soviet telepathic experiment. The sense impressions were overwhelming. When the phosphene activity ended, I felt that I had regained my sight, that I had been blind all my life ana could see for the first time. What I saw, then, were the mysteries of the universe; and at the same time a theophany took place, the mani¬ festation of a divine entity which passed into my own mind, displacing my ego and becoming a theolepsy. From there on, in visions, dialog and dreams, it informed me of all things which were: the actual or absolute reality I had been seeking for 25 years. One of the first actions this

= what happened to me in 3-74 was that for some reason (e.g. (1) orthomolecular vitamins (2) I was possessed by the mimicker) I could see the mimicker & its mimickry. So in a way, what nearly happened is very simple. Always it strives to cause its intrusions etc to appear part of the normal world ; I would guess that it occupied me briefly in order to cause me to do certain good things I would not on my own have done - & then departed, preferring us to have autonomy. It works principally on our percept system, so that we think we see the phenomenal things (but they are actually Dikos, alive, part of the mimicking organism). It switches my 1974 personality with an AD 70 personality. the lovely AI voice, however, was its - it can enter us & imitate us (to other people), but I, Being occupied, knew it, without understanding it.

This is a very high order of mimickery by a tutulary entity that loves us; it has knowledge but no direct power- except that it can effect the causal chains for a Good, purposeful outcome by occupying (assimilating) this or that object, not necessarily permanently. Rather than calling it God I'd prefer to call it a higher life form.

Point: God does not normally exist in the phenomenal world, because that world is not real (cf Parmenides, Susan Langer & Kant). We perceive it, however, as real for philosophically documented reasons. However, on occasion God can advance (intrude?)

what I find myself seeing is Acts; meanwhile inwardly I experience his knowledge, recognition + fear of this world + the romans, his knowledge of secret early xtian codes + sacraments, the Koine - everything. Could I reasonably assume he + his world were real, whereas I + mine were not? Obviously, given our internal differences - + different perceived external worlds - I could argue that one of us + his world was the real person + world, + the other was not. But that would be arbitrary. On what basis would I make this judgment? Especially, why would I pick his over mine, + him over me? This, in effect, is what I have done. "I got into Thomas' head, experienced him as psyche + his world; he + it are different from me + mine, + he + it are real (correct, absolute, objective) but I + mine - we're just 'aspects' i.e. phantoms". This does not follow. I have been governed too much by my own fictional models (e.g. "Maze", "Joint"). In actuality, all I've really established is total subjectivity of world, related to - locked into - self. I just happen to house two selves in my head. Each with his own subjective world. What makes it so startling is that each of these two selves lives in a world thousands of years + miles from the other's, + is quite at home there, in each case in his here + his now. This is what topples our conception of space + time, +, finally, world itself. My faulty reasoning is based on Kant's distinction between the phenomenal world + the Dinge-an-sich; to wit: 1) What we see as the phenomenal world is

It is not enough to DISMISS time, space, causality + the phenomenal world as irreal; two questions remain: 1) If they are irreal, why do we experience them? Why should an organism arise that perceives things which don't exist? 2) What is real? It cannot actually be said that we can't know something; all that can be said is that we don't know how to know it (I am thinking of Kant's Dinge-an-sich), no one can claim to know what can't be known. (Note) Regarding (1): we are locked into this irreal structure by Zebra, which creates it to maintain organization + functioning of its parts. Zebra is not interested in making us aware of our actual situation but only in organization + functioning; these irreal elements are essentially expedients - the criterion is use (i.e. a pragmatic one) not veracity, activity not knowledge. Regarding (2): see preceding 10,000 pages. The Kantian unknowable can berhaps be discerned [...] detected by the perturbations it creates in the phenomenal world. If it creates none (but it does) probably it is not there. What we need to do is follow Hume's careful scrutinizing method, not German double abstractions based on Hindu assetions about the ultimate unknowability of reality behing appearance. (Note) To preserve us as functioning parts it is preserving our integrity, our health + our physical [nee]ds; the deluding serves our biological but not our purely intellectual needs; the first outweighs the 2nd.

Zebra is the true koinos kosmos, + each of our push-pull psyche-world locked-together systems is idios kosmos, + there are billions of them. folder 02 - 060
called into being as an expression of a non-physical entity: pure knowing. Noos does not know because it possesses a brain, but rather brain (physical universe) exists to serve the Noos, which has generated world but is not dependent on it to be. (Noos is hierarchically prior to brain.) However, it uses the brain to lay out its plan - uses it as a map topologically congruent to its own structure (nature). To see the brain is to see Noos expressed as reality. (It could generate another brain if this one failed.) The brain, then, is a print out of Noos + bonded to it as one entity with two aspects: physical + process of the physical. Process is hierarchically prior. The brain can best be viewed in terms of what it does, rather than is. Its life is the flux of arrangement: its interconnections or structure. If we could see it in its totality we would see something like an intricate, very beautiful single geometric shape in polychrome, utilizing ratio (proportion, intervals, _octaves_) to achieve its integral harmonie. I believe the proportional basis of it to be 1:.618034, through which it replicates itself down through its levels (orders of organization); by this contstant it maintains its design throughout itself. Thus every individual bit of it recapitulates the totality, + from any bit the totality can be apprehended. Therefore I say the totality is _not_ unknowable (contrary to what Kant teaches): viz: any given bit is so to speak a diagram of the whole circuit.

The outer, then, is Plato's cave with its images, + the inner is ontologically real (but unrecognized as such even by Kant as the true locus of the Real). (But my writing so recognizes it.) The inner projects the images onto the outer, as my system states, + the push-pull enhancing sets in, but: the origin is in P.'s inner space: two mirrors are involved, but - the inner space (+ world!) has priority over the lower, outer. Mastery, as Caesar has, in the lower, outer has no meaning, since the lower, outer is maya - a mere shadow, without substance. He is ruler of a phantom realm, which however, beguiles + seduces us, + causes us to forget the upper, inner. (Pain is one one of its powers.) Art, then, is a transfer of the inner realm of a given person -his ontology- to others through the outer, but it isn't part of the outer (it is an inner brain print; esp. Beethoven's + Wagner's + Mahler's music), the more "subjective" art is, the more real it is. S-F, then, is potentially a higher art than is generally realized since it can be highly "subjective" (more accurately inner.) (The inner landscape.) (+ the greatest novels would be "Remembrance of Things Past" + most of all "Ulysses.") Well, this analysis confers very high value to my writing, esp "Stigmata" + "Ubik" + "MITHC" The inference of all this is that Caesar is going to appear -externally, obviously, evidentally- to be in charge, but in a latent, subtle way in this inner-outer dialectic (which may be the dialectic I saw in 3-74!) his victories are losses + his power defeated - not at the end of time but during the course of the dialectic itself, during the process. He is undermined somehow.

It now becomes possible to identify the Kingdom level #3 with Kant's "Dinge-an-sich" in contrast to level #1, the phenomenal world! This is a teriffic step, for if (as it appears) Christ reigns in the Platonist real world, then it (the rule) has an absolute quality, + since, as is well known, we can't see the real world, this is why we can't see the Kingdom. But it "perturbates" our merely simulated #1 world: faint echoes, images + reflections, dimly seen, + in reverse (v. supra). This explains the mysterious answers Christ gives to everyone as to where his kingdom is: the laws of physics then as now (cosmology) could not explain it. Our Newtonian cosmology can't either -which is why I remain perplexed- e.g. as to the syntonic nature of level #3 with #1, etc. It is a mode of being-in-the-world, with "world" as function of psyche, in the push-pull system I envision. The latent + immortal (transtemporal or total present) psyche must manifest itself, replacing the normal limited-present temporal psyche. In "Tears" these shifting, syntonic "aspects" replace one another, + "Tears" winds up as a verbal description of the Kingdom, not just of Acts. Now it is possible absolutely to equate Zebra with Christ. I saw Zebra; I saw the mystical unincarnated Christ or Holy Wisdom itself: the Logos (Ubik). I have "triangulated" my fix. I had entered the latent world - but also I realized he is here, camouflaged, modulating people, processes + objects, just as all aspects of the latent worlds are hidden. He is the most important. __ Man as magic micro mirror of the macrocosm, reflects (+ hence contains) the map (or Logos) of the macrocosm replicated in miniature (cf. Bruno + Hussey on Heraclitus). He contains the cosmos by containing this map or plan or Logos of it; that's how it works! And since the cosmos is alive + thinks, the map is alive and thinks. Thus he finds the latent realm in him primarily, awareness of the latent realm is a form of self-awareness: of who he really is. (Citizen of Acts world.) Obviously he is able to see the latent world because of a change in him.

have us experience; + 5) He chooses different aspects (i.e. different spaces + different times) for different people. So here is a qualified Berkeleyian idealism. God impinges -not directly on our senses the impression that objective reality exists- but imparts a modifying grid inserted between our percept system + objective reality so designed + in such a manner that we experience world differently from one another - + the crucial difference has to do with precisely the two categories which Kant asserted were our subjective ontological matrices: time + space. Kant is right; time + space are not properties of the Dinge-an-sich. But also they are not due to us, arising from us or by us; they are imposed on us by the creator. Whether this is "good" or "bad" I do not know, but thus it is. God imposes the ordering matrices, not us + not objective world. Has anyone thought of a triune division? Viz: {illustration of 3 text boxes with arrows going from right to left, from "objective world" to "God" to "us"} {note in the margin:} see p. 131

This is how Thomas can be in my head but be in another space + time from me. His God-imposed grid differs from mine. Reality filtered through God!

The import of this is so colossal as to stagger me. Berkeley is wrong; objective world exists. But our immediate + primary experience is not of it -though it exists- but of God or anyhow a volitional entity with the power of determining when we are + where we are - which, as Kant showed, is the whole essence of percept-data ordering (hypostasis). Power of this order equals, for all practical purposes, total power over us. Now, what if this ordering, this grid, is occlusive vis-a-vis ourselves? Then the situation is deadly, + we do need "medical help"! What if the Gnostics + Mani are right? What if this grid is a bilking of us? A making us prisoners? Because if this constitutes a prison, this filtering, then the Gnostic "metaphysical strike" makes a lot of sense. + since J was obviously insane, though brilliant, then our "reality" is odrered by a psychotic God who is into random creating + destroying - i.e. without purpose or pattern, as his sick mind directs (v. that Catherist poem!) a fevered mind! On the other hand, this disclosure, although of titanic epistemological significance in itself, has equally titanic implications vis-a-vis this whole homoplasmate situation. The plasmate -i.e. the macrobrain- now is seen as the actual reality in which we dwell, whether naturally so or as an

+ it is over this inner universe the real one, that Christ rules!

The outside world is a copy of people's minds, the neumenal or real world lies - not behind it as Plato + Kant supposed - but within us, I have figured out where Christ's Kingdom is, + it is another + unsuspected universe. But to understand how it can be, you have to realize we are stationary + fed takes.

+ with this inside universe we are not alone; we are the multi-self entity the cosmic Christ who gathers us all, living + dead, to him as a single corpus (sentient, world-creating God-thing).

So not only only did I (after long rejecting the irreal "external" universe) find the real, inner one, but I found the "Kingdom of Heaven" where Christ rules. In story upon novel I dematerialize the outer universe - until at last into the vacuum there flooded all at once the truly real, made up of boundless vast spacial geometric conceptions, wisdom, + ancient eternal healing entities.

This reveals the plurality world for what it is: an epiphenomenon of mind! If it wasn't, my mind could not have affected/altered it!

5:30 a.m. Each human brain is a different universe, literally, not metaphorically: vast spaces. I saw mine (i.e. my brain). Hermetic alchemy. So the vast spaces that I saw was my own inner space projected outward; it is greater than outer space.

I was interacting with reality at its deepest level below that of the plural epiphenomena; I joined with it (or became aware that I was already joined with it). It took the form of an open system biological organism model because it is; this is why it could interpenetrate me and me it. I can never know the not-me greater mind as it is in itself, since when I encounter it I actively participate in shaping the aspect it shows me. I do not experience it; I experience myself merged with it in syzygy. This is the issue Kant raised regarding the thing-in-itself; his arguments hold true here. This other mind probably appears only to me under the aspect I encountered; thus I can say little or nothing about its intrinsic nature. This is what has wrecked my attempt to analyze it for these six years; I overlooked the fact that I was a participant in it and not a detached observer outside it. I changed it by encountering it. It is significant that the boundaries of my mind and its mind are lost in such an encounter; we blur together into the syzygy. I'd like to conclude that this indicates isomorphism, but it does not. Nor can I even be sure which parts (elements, aspects) are from its mind and which are from mine. All I can be sure of is: it was not all me.

developed his Logos idea to explain that Plato's eide exist in a mind, and that mind Philo called Logos rather than Nous; further, in 3-74 1 saw the macrometasomakosmos and it looked to be made up of abstract Forms --which I called phylogons-- indicating that what I saw existed in a mind, and that mind was Valis or was Valis' depending on whether Valis has a physical existence or not; so I did see what Philo was talking about; I did, then, see the Logos). Does this mean that our universe was never real in the first place (shades of Gnostic acosmism!) I don't know what to make of it. Plato, I guess, was right; the flux world isn't really; nothing in it has einai; it never truly is; it is always becoming but never is. But the Logos is, I mean the macrometasomakosmos. Is that why the Logos invaded our universe (I feel that it did, and this accords with Scripture)? It is the real life form on this planet, and it remembers everything that ever happened anywhere on this planet; it has all this stored as information. Thus it contains the entire history of this planet in a perpetual now. What is it? Where did it come from? How long has it been here? Why is it here? Why doesn't anyone but me know about it? Is it the Logos? If not -- it not only remembers everything that happens, it controls the flow of these happenings by means of its control of causation and its engramming of human minds subliminally, so it does more than remember; it causes to be what comes to pass. Everything that happens in our flux world is its internal information metabolism. We see this internal information metabolism only partially; there is a whole "second signal" to which we are occluded; were we to see it completely we would see the activity of a macrobrain, which we are in. Also, its time-rate is different from ours; it is related to time in a way totally different from the way we are. I am not sure it is in time at all; what we call "time" may be --as Kant says-- an ordering structuring of our own minds, not in the world itself. Nothing passes away for it; all is retained, but all is structured. It is --I keep saying this-- it is attacking, defeating, dismantling and assimilating our universe --the not it-- piece by piece, placing each piece in the exact spot of its own metasoma that it wants it, a vast kosmos in Pythagoras' sense. Its information traffic pours around us, only dimly seen by us; more: it uses our information communications media as a carrier -- unknown to us, occluded from us. Does it occlude us? And if so, why? Why doesn't it want us to know it is here?
